NKJV.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,
NKJV.2: To Timothy, a beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
NKJV.3: I thank God, whom I serve with a pure conscience, as my forefathers did, as without ceasing I remember you in my prayers night and day,
NKJV.4: greatly desiring to see you, being mindful of your tears, that I may be filled with joy,
NKJV.5: when I call to remembrance the genuine faith that is in you, which dwelt first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am persuaded is in you also.
NKJV.6: Therefore I remind you to stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
NKJV.7: For God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power and of love and of a sound mind.
NKJV.8: Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me His prisoner, but share with me in the sufferings for the gospel according to the power of God,
NKJV.9: who has saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to His own purpose and grace which was given to us in Christ Jesus before time began,
NKJV.10: but has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior Jesus Christ, who has abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel,
NKJV.11: to which I was appointed a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.
NKJV.12: For this reason I also suffer these things; nevertheless I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have believed and am persuaded that He is able to keep what I have committed to Him until that Day.
NKJV.13: Hold fast the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me, in faith and love which are in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.14: That good thing which was committed to you, keep by the Holy Spirit who dwells in us.
NKJV.15: This you know, that all those in Asia have turned away from me, among whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.
NKJV.16: The Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain;
NKJV.17: but when he arrived in Rome, he sought me out very zealously and found me.
NKJV.18: The Lord grant to him that he may find mercy from the Lord in that Day—and you know very well how many ways he ministered to me at Ephesus.

ESV.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God according to the promise of the life that is in Christ Jesus,
ESV.2: To Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
ESV.3: I thank God whom I serve, as did my ancestors, with a clear conscience, as I remember you constantly in my prayers night and day.
ESV.4: As I remember your tears, I long to see you, that I may be filled with joy.
ESV.5: I am reminded of your sincere faith, a faith that dwelt first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice and now, I am sure, dwells in you as well.
ESV.6: For this reason I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God, which is in you through the laying on of my hands,
ESV.7: for God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control.
ESV.8: Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony about our Lord, nor of me his prisoner, but share in suffering for the gospel by the power of God,
ESV.9: who saved us and called us to  a holy calling, not because of our works but because of his own purpose and grace, which he gave us in Christ Jesus before the ages began,
ESV.10: and which now has been manifested through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel,
ESV.11: for which I was appointed a preacher and apostle and teacher,
ESV.12: which is why I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have believed, and I am convinced that he is able to guard until that day what has been entrusted to me.
ESV.13: Follow the pattern of the sound  words that you have heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
ESV.14: By the Holy Spirit who dwells within us, guard the good deposit entrusted to you.
ESV.15: You are aware that all who are in Asia turned away from me, among whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
ESV.16: May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chains,
ESV.17: but when he arrived in Rome he searched for me earnestly and found me —
ESV.18: may the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that day! — and you well know all the service he rendered at Ephesus.

NASB.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, according to the promise of life in Christ Jesus,
NASB.2: To Timothy, my beloved son: Grace, mercy [and] peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
NASB.3: I thank God, whom I serve with a clear conscience the way my forefathers did, as I constantly remember you in my prayers night and day,
NASB.4: longing to see you, even as I recall your tears, so that I may be filled with joy.
NASB.5: For I am mindful of the sincere faith within you, which first dwelt in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am sure that [it is] in you as well.
NASB.6: For this reason I remind you to kindle afresh the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
NASB.7: For God has not given us a spirit of timidity, but of power and love and discipline.
NASB.8: Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord or of me His prisoner, but join with [me] in suffering for the gospel according to the power of God,
NASB.9: who has saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to His own purpose and grace which was granted us in Christ Jesus from all eternity,
NASB.10: but now has been revealed by the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel,
NASB.11: for which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle and a teacher.
NASB.12: For this reason I also suffer these things, but I am not ashamed; for I know whom I have believed and I am convinced that He is able to guard what I have entrusted to Him until that day.
NASB.13: Retain the standard of sound words which you have heard from me, in the faith and love which are in Christ Jesus.
NASB.14: Guard, through the Holy Spirit who dwells in us, the treasure which has been entrusted to [you].
NASB.15: You are aware of the fact that all who are in Asia turned away from me, among whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
NASB.16: The Lord grant mercy to the house of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chains;
NASB.17: but when he was in Rome, he eagerly searched for me and found me--
NASB.18: the Lord grant to him to find mercy from the Lord on that day-- and you know very well what services he rendered at Ephesus.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, chosen by the will of God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. I have been sent out to tell others about the life he has promised through faith in Christ Jesus.
NLT.2: I am writing to Timothy, my dear son. May God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord give you grace, mercy, and peace.
NLT.3: Timothy, I thank God for you — the God I serve with a clear conscience, just as my ancestors did. Night and day I constantly remember you in my prayers.
NLT.4: I long to see you again, for I remember your tears as we parted. And I will be filled with joy when we are together again.
NLT.5: I remember your genuine faith, for you share the faith that first filled your grandmother Lois and your mother, Eunice. And I know that same faith continues strong in you.
NLT.6: This is why I remind you to fan into flames the spiritual gift God gave you when I laid my hands on you.
NLT.7: For God has not given us a spirit of fear and timidity, but of power, love, and self-discipline.
NLT.8: So never be ashamed to tell others about our Lord. And don’t be ashamed of me, either, even though I’m in prison for him. With the strength God gives you, be ready to suffer with me for the sake of the Good News.
NLT.9: For God saved us and called us to live a holy life. He did this, not because we deserved it, but because that was his plan from before the beginning of time — to show us his grace through Christ Jesus.
NLT.10: And now he has made all of this plain to us by the appearing of Christ Jesus, our Savior. He broke the power of death and illuminated the way to life and immortality through the Good News.
NLT.11: And God chose me to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of this Good News.
NLT.12: That is why I am suffering here in prison. But I am not ashamed of it, for I know the one in whom I trust, and I am sure that he is able to guard what I have entrusted to him until the day of his return.
NLT.13: Hold on to the pattern of wholesome teaching you learned from me — a pattern shaped by the faith and love that you have in Christ Jesus.
NLT.14: Through the power of the Holy Spirit who lives within us, carefully guard the precious truth that has been entrusted to you.
NLT.15: As you know, everyone from the province of Asia has deserted me — even Phygelus and Hermogenes.
NLT.16: May the Lord show special kindness to Onesiphorus and all his family because he often visited and encouraged me. He was never ashamed of me because I was in chains.
NLT.17: When he came to Rome, he searched everywhere until he found me.
NLT.18: May the Lord show him special kindness on the day of Christ’s return. And you know very well how helpful he was in Ephesus.

WEB.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, according to the promise of the life which is in Christ Jesus,
WEB.2: to Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
WEB.3: I thank God, whom I serve as my forefathers did, with a pure conscience. How unceasing is my memory of you in my petitions, night and day
WEB.4: longing to see you, remembering your tears, that I may be filled with joy;
WEB.5: having been reminded of the sincere faith that is in you; which lived first in your grandmother Lois, and your mother Eunice, and, I am persuaded, in you also.
WEB.6: For this cause, I remind you that you should stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
WEB.7: For God didn’t give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control.
WEB.8: Therefore don’t be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner; but endure hardship for the Good News according to the power of God,
WEB.9: who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before times eternal,
WEB.10: but has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus, who abolished death, and brought life and immortality to light through the Good News.
WEB.11: For this, I was appointed as a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.
WEB.12: For this cause I also suffer these things. Yet I am not ashamed, for I know him whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that he is able to guard that which I have committed to him against that day.
WEB.13: Hold the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
WEB.14: That good thing which was committed to you, guard through the Holy Spirit who dwells in us.
WEB.15: This you know, that all who are in Asia turned away from me; of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
WEB.16: May the Lord grant mercy to the house of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain,
WEB.17: but when he was in Rome, he sought me diligently, and found me
WEB.18: (the Lord grant to him to find the Lord’s mercy in that day); and in how many things he served at Ephesus, you know very well.

CSB17.1: Paul, <sup>ⓜ</sup> an apostle of Christ Jesus by God’s will, <sup>ⓝ</sup> for the sake of the promise of life <sup>ⓐ</sup> in Christ Jesus:
CSB17.2: To Timothy, my dearly loved son. Grace, mercy, and peace <sup>ⓑ</sup> from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
CSB17.3: I thank God, whom I serve with a clear conscience <sup>ⓒ</sup> as my ancestors did, when I constantly remember you in my prayers night and day. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.4: Remembering your tears, I long to see you so that I may be filled with joy.
CSB17.5: I recall your sincere faith <sup>ⓔ</sup> that first lived in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice <sup>ⓕ</sup> and now, I am convinced, is in you also.
CSB17.6: Therefore, I remind you to rekindle the gift of God that is in you through the laying on of my hands. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.7: For God has not given us a spirit of fear, <sup>ⓗ</sup> but one of power, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> love, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and sound judgment.
CSB17.8: So don’t be ashamed <sup>ⓚ</sup> of the testimony about our Lord, <sup>ⓛ</sup> or of me his prisoner. <sup>ⓜ</sup> Instead, share in suffering for the gospel, relying on the power <sup>ⓝ</sup> of God.
CSB17.9: He has saved us and called us with a holy calling, <sup>ⓐ</sup> not according to our works, <sup>ⓑ</sup> but according to his own purpose <sup>ⓒ</sup> and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before time began.
CSB17.10: This has now been made evident through the appearing <sup>ⓓ</sup> of our Savior Christ Jesus, who has abolished death <sup>ⓔ</sup> and has brought life <sup>ⓕ</sup> and immortality to light through the gospel.
CSB17.11: For this gospel I was appointed <sup>ⓖ</sup> a herald, apostle, and teacher, <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.12: and that is why I suffer <sup>ⓗ</sup> these things. But I am not ashamed, because I know whom I have believed and am persuaded that he is able to guard what has been entrusted to me <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> until that day. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.13: Hold on to the pattern of sound teaching <sup>ⓚ</sup> that you have heard from me, <sup>ⓛ</sup> in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
CSB17.14: Guard the good deposit through the Holy Spirit who lives in us.
CSB17.15: You know that all those in the province of Asia <sup>ⓜ</sup> have deserted me, <sup>ⓝ</sup> including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
CSB17.16: May the Lord grant mercy <sup>ⓐ</sup> to the household of Onesiphorus, <sup>ⓑ</sup> because he often refreshed me <sup>ⓒ</sup> and was not ashamed of my chains. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.17: On the contrary, when he was in Rome, <sup>ⓔ</sup> he diligently searched for me and found me.
CSB17.18: May the Lord grant that he obtain mercy <sup>ⓕ</sup> from him on that day. You know very well how much he ministered at Ephesus. <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: Paul, an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Christ Jesus (the Messiah, the Anointed) by the will of God, according to the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus,
AMP.2: to Timothy, my beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
AMP.3: I thank God, whom I worship and serve with a clear conscience the way my forefathers did, as I constantly remember you in my prayers night and day,
AMP.4: and as I recall your tears, I long to see you so that I may be filled with joy.
AMP.5: I remember your sincere and unqualified faith [the surrendering of your entire self to God in Christ with confident trust in His power, wisdom and goodness, a faith] which first lived in [the heart of] your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am confident that it is in you as well.
AMP.6: That is why I remind you to fan into flame the gracious gift of God, [that inner fire—the special endowment] which is in you through the laying on of my hands [with those of the elders at your ordination].
AMP.7: For God did not give us a spirit of timidity or cowardice or fear, but [He has given us a spirit] of power and of love and of sound judgment and personal discipline [abilities that result in a calm, well-balanced mind and self-control].
AMP.8: So do not be ashamed to testify about our Lord or about me His prisoner, but with me take your share of suffering for the gospel [continue to preach regardless of the circumstances], in accordance with the power of God [for His power is invincible],
AMP.9: for He delivered us and saved us and called us with a holy calling [a calling that leads to a consecrated life—a life set apart—a life of purpose], not because of our works [or because of any personal merit—we could do nothing to earn this], but because of His own purpose and grace [His amazing, undeserved favor] which was granted to us in Christ Jesus before the world began [eternal ages ago],
AMP.10: but now [that extraordinary purpose and grace] has been fully disclosed and realized by us through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus who [through His incarnation and earthly ministry] abolished death [making it null and void] and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel,
AMP.11: for which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle and a teacher [of this good news regarding salvation].
AMP.12: This is why I suffer as I do. Still, I am not ashamed; for I know Him [and I am personally acquainted with Him] whom I have believed [with absolute trust and confidence in Him and in the truth of His deity], and I am persuaded [beyond any doubt] that He is able to guard that which I have entrusted to Him until that day [when I stand before Him].
AMP.13: Keep and follow the pattern of sound teaching (doctrine) which you have heard from me, in the faith and love which are in Christ Jesus.
AMP.14: Guard [with greatest care] and keep unchanged, the treasure [that precious truth] which has been entrusted to you [that is, the good news about salvation through personal faith in Christ Jesus], through [the help of] the Holy Spirit who dwells in us.
AMP.15: You are aware of the fact that all who are in [the province of] Asia turned away and deserted me, Phygelus and Hermogenes among them.
AMP.16: The Lord grant mercy to the family of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and showed me kindness [comforting and reviving me like fresh air] and he was not ashamed of my chains [for Christ's sake];
AMP.17: but [instead] when he reached Rome, he eagerly searched for me and found me—
AMP.18: the Lord grant to him that he may find mercy from the Lord on that [great] day. You know very well how many things he did for me and what a help he was at Ephesus [you know better than I can tell you].

MSG.1: I, Paul, am on special assignment for Christ, carrying out God's plan laid out in the Message of Life by Jesus.
MSG.2: I write this to you, Timothy, the son I love so much. All the best from our God and Christ be yours!
MSG.3: Every time I say your name in prayer—which is practically all the time—I thank God for you, the God I worship with my whole life in the tradition of my ancestors.
MSG.4: I miss you a lot, especially when I remember that last tearful good-bye, and I look forward to a joy-packed reunion.
MSG.5: That precious memory triggers another: your honest faith—and what a rich faith it is, handed down from your grandmother Lois to your mother Eunice, and now to you!
MSG.6: And the special gift of ministry you received when I laid hands on you and prayed—keep that ablaze!
MSG.7: God doesn't want us to be shy with his gifts, but bold and loving and sensible.
MSG.8: So don't be embarrassed to speak up for our Master or for me, his prisoner. Take your share of suffering for the Message along with the rest of us. We can only keep on going, after all, by the power of God,
MSG.9: who first saved us and then called us to this holy work. We had nothing to do with it. It was all his idea, a gift prepared for us in Jesus long before we knew anything about it.
MSG.10: But we know it now. Since the appearance of our Savior, nothing could be plainer: death defeated, life vindicated in a steady blaze of light, all through the work of Jesus.
MSG.11: This is the Message I've been set apart to proclaim as preacher, emissary, and teacher.
MSG.12: It's also the cause of all this trouble I'm in. But I have no regrets. I couldn't be more sure of my ground—the One I've trusted in can take care of what he's trusted me to do right to the end.
MSG.13: So keep at your work, this faith and love rooted in Christ, exactly as I set it out for you. It's as sound as the day you first heard it from me.
MSG.14: Guard this precious thing placed in your custody by the Holy Spirit who works in us.
MSG.15: I'm sure you know by now that everyone in the province of Asia deserted me, even Phygelus and Hermogenes.
MSG.16: But God bless Onesiphorus and his family! Many's the time I've been refreshed in that house. And he wasn't embarrassed a bit that I was in jail.
MSG.17: The first thing he did when he got to Rome was look me up.
MSG.18: May God on the Last Day treat him as well as he treated me. And then there was all the help he provided in Ephesus—but you know that better than I.

NIV.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, according to the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus,
NIV.2:
To Timothy, my dear son:
Grace, mercy and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
NIV.3:
Encouragement to Be Faithful
I thank God, whom I serve, as my forefathers did, with a clear conscience, as night and day I constantly remember you in my prayers.
NIV.4: Recalling your tears, I long to see you, so that I may be filled with joy.
NIV.5: I have been reminded of your sincere faith, which first lived in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice and, I am persuaded, now lives in you also.
NIV.6: For this reason I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God, which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
NIV.7: For God did not give us a spirit of timidity, but a spirit of power, of love and of self-discipline.
NIV.8: So do not be ashamed to testify about our Lord, or ashamed of me his prisoner. But join with me in suffering for the gospel, by the power of God,
NIV.9: who has saved us and called us to a holy life—not because of anything we have done but because of his own purpose and grace. This grace was given us in Christ Jesus before the beginning of time,
NIV.10: but it has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus, who has destroyed death and has brought life and immortality to light through the gospel.
NIV.11: And of this gospel I was appointed a herald and an apostle and a teacher.
NIV.12: That is why I am suffering as I am. Yet I am not ashamed, because I know whom I have believed, and am convinced that he is able to guard what I have entrusted to him for that day.
NIV.13: What you heard from me, keep as the pattern of sound teaching, with faith and love in Christ Jesus.
NIV.14: Guard the good deposit that was entrusted to you—guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit who lives in us.
NIV.15: You know that everyone in the province of Asia has deserted me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
NIV.16: May the Lord show mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chains.
NIV.17: On the contrary, when he was in Rome, he searched hard for me until he found me.
NIV.18: May the Lord grant that he will find mercy from the Lord on that day! You know very well in how many ways he helped me in Ephesus.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, for the sake of the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus,
NRSVCE.2: To Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
NRSVCE.3: I am grateful to God — whom I worship with a clear conscience, as my ancestors did — when I remember you constantly in my prayers night and day.
NRSVCE.4: Recalling your tears, I long to see you so that I may be filled with joy.
NRSVCE.5: I am reminded of your sincere faith, a faith that lived first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice and now, I am sure, lives in you.
NRSVCE.6: For this reason I remind you to rekindle the gift of God that is within you through the laying on of my hands;
NRSVCE.7: for God did not give us a spirit of cowardice, but rather a spirit of power and of love and of self-discipline.
NRSVCE.8: Do not be ashamed, then, of the testimony about our Lord or of me his prisoner, but join with me in suffering for the gospel, relying on the power of God,
NRSVCE.9: who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works but according to his own purpose and grace. This grace was given to us in Christ Jesus before the ages began,
NRSVCE.10: but it has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel.
NRSVCE.11: For this gospel I was appointed a herald and an apostle and a teacher,
NRSVCE.12: and for this reason I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed, for I know the one in whom I have put my trust, and I am sure that he is able to guard until that day what I have entrusted to him.
NRSVCE.13: Hold to the standard of sound teaching that you have heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.14: Guard the good treasure entrusted to you, with the help of the Holy Spirit living in us.
NRSVCE.15: You are aware that all who are in Asia have turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
NRSVCE.16: May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain;
NRSVCE.17: when he arrived in Rome, he eagerly searched for me and found me
NRSVCE.18: — may the Lord grant that he will find mercy from the Lord on that day! And you know very well how much service he rendered in Ephesus.

NET.1: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, to further the promise of life in Christ Jesus,
NET.2: to Timothy, my dear child. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord!
NET.3: I am thankful to God, whom I have served with a clear conscience as my ancestors did, when I remember you in my prayers as I do constantly night and day.
NET.4: As I remember your tears, I long to see you, so that I may be filled with joy.
NET.5: I recall your sincere faith that was alive first in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice, and I am sure is in you.
NET.6: Because of this I remind you to rekindle God’s gift that you possess through the laying on of my hands.
NET.7: For God did not give us a Spirit of fear but of power and love and self-control.
NET.8: So do not be ashamed of the testimony about our Lord or of me, a prisoner for his sake, but by God’s power accept your share of suffering for the gospel.
NET.9: He is the one who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not based on our works but on his own purpose and grace, granted to us in Christ Jesus before time began,
NET.10: but now made visible through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus. He has broken the power of death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel!
NET.11: For this gospel I was appointed a preacher and apostle and teacher.
NET.12: Because of this, in fact, I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed, because I know the one in whom my faith is set and I am convinced that he is able to protect what has been entrusted to me until that day.
NET.13: Hold to the standard of sound words that you heard from me and do so with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
NET.14: Protect that good thing entrusted to you, through the Holy Spirit who lives within us.
NET.15: You know that everyone in the province of Asia deserted me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
NET.16: May the Lord grant mercy to the family of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my imprisonment.
NET.17: But when he arrived in Rome, he eagerly searched for me and found me.
NET.18: May the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that day! And you know very well all the ways he served me in Ephesus.
